definition: VP's CMP_MAP gradient retains seeds making progress toward the target despite I2S substitution actively biasing the corpus away from it (by substituting competing literals or anchoring a field to plausible recorded values), creating a unique cmplog-as-loser pattern.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: VP gradient rewards upstream comparisons leading to MODI script selection; cmplog's I2S substitutes competing script codepoint literals, steering the derived script field away from MODI and polluting the corpus.
signature.one_line: VP gradient escapes I2S corpus pollution toward derived MODI script value

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must parse into a font whose buffer->props.script equals HB_SCRIPT_MODI so the MODI case-arm is selected in the script dispatch switch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes that survive harfbuzz font parsing far enough to set buffer->props.script = HB_SCRIPT_MODI before reaching the switch at hb-ot-shaper-vowel-constraints.cc:56. The script field is populated by upstream font/cmap/feature processing from input bytes; the blocker case-arm fires only when that derived field equals the MODI constant.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Naive (Pair 1 winner) reaches MODI 10/10 trials via random havoc on a font-shaped corpus (winner seeds carry an OTTO TrueType magic header and a `df 20 20` payload that drives script selection toward MODI). value_profile_cmplog (Pair 2 winner) reaches MODI 9/10 trials because the added value-profile gradient feedback rewards seeds that get closer to MODI in upstream comparisons, escaping the corpus state cmplog gets trapped in.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks 8/10 trials because its input-to-state substitution mutator harvests the literal codepoint constants from the inner switches of OTHER scripts (DEVANAGARI 0x0905/0x0906/0x0909/0x090F/0x0930, TELUGU 0x0C12/0x0C3F/0x0C46/0x0C4A, MALAYALAM 0x0D07/0x0D09/0x0D0E/0x0D12, TAKRI 0x11680/0x11686). The loser overlay shows cmplog explores those competing case-arms heavily (15 traversals of each inner switch) but never lands on MODI: the input-to-state substitution pulls inputs toward non-MODI script values, polluting the corpus away from the blocker.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Pattern B (input-to-state substitution hurts). The MODI case-arm is not a literal in the input — buffer->props.script is a derived field set by upstream font parsing. cmplog's input-to-state mutator finds many literal codepoints in the inner switch CMPs of other scripts and substitutes them, but those substitutions push the derived script field toward DEVANAGARI/TELUGU/MALAYALAM/TAKRI rather than MODI. naive avoids this trap by doing pure random byte mutations (no CMP-directed pollution), and vpc escapes it via the added value-profile gradient over upstream comparisons that lead to MODI selection.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the MODI case-arm of the script dispatch switch in _hb_preprocess_text_vowel_constraints.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Location: /src/harfbuzz/src/hb-ot-shaper-vowel-constraints.cc:426:5

evidence_trail.claim: Naive resolves the MODI branch in all 10 trials while cmplog blocks in 8 of 10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: naive                           10        0          0  winner (I2S vs cmplog)

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog is the loser in both decisive pairs, blocking 8/10 trials.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: cmplog                           2        8          0  loser (I2S vs naive); loser (value_profile vs value_profile_cmplog)

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog wins Pair 2 over cmplog under the value_profile delta.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: --- Pair 2: value_profile_cmplog > cmplog  [delta: value_profile] ---

evidence_trail.claim: Only the winner-side seeds annotate the MODI case-arm body; loser seeds skip it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: [W]   426      case HB_SCRIPT_MODI: <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The per-branch divergence on MODI's case-arm: winner takes T=10 F=0, loser takes T=0 F=9.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 426  T=10 F=0  T=0 F=9  case HB_SCRIPT_MODI:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser seeds heavily explore the DEVANAGARI case-arm inner switch instead of MODI.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L  58  T=0 F=10  T=1 F=8  case HB_SCRIPT_DEVANAGARI:

evidence_trail.claim: Loser also enters the inner switches of TELUGU, MALAYALAM, and TAKRI but never reaches MODI.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 222  T=0 F=10  T=1 F=8  case HB_SCRIPT_TELUGU:

evidence_trail.claim: Loser explores the DEVANAGARI inner codepoint switch 15 times without matching MODI codepoints.
Winner seeds include the TrueType `OTTO` magic header at offset 0 plus a `df 20 20` payload that drives script selection.
cmplog's mutator stack includes I2SRandReplace which substitutes CMP operands into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Mutators**: naive's havoc + token stack **plus** `I2SRandReplace`.

evidence_trail.claim: I2SRandReplace harvests operands by scanning the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substituting matched byte sequences in the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient feedback on top of cmplog's instrumentation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Instrumentation**: union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic harness with a single switch on a derived-from-input field, where the I2S dictionary at the cmplog mutator harvests N>=8 distinct competing literal arms, fails to show cmplog underperforming naive on the target arm (i.e., I2S does NOT cause directional pollution under controlled chain depth), then the I2S-poisoning explanation is wrong and the cmplog deficit must come from somewhere else (e.g., per-execution cost slowing cmplog throughput).

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The chain from input bytes to buffer->props.script is opaque in this prompt — we infer from DIVERGENT BRANCHES that cmplog's mutations push the script field toward DEVANAGARI/TELUGU/MALAYALAM rather than MODI, but we have not traced exactly which input bytes the harfbuzz font parser consumes to set the script field, so the 'I2S substitutes competing codepoints' story is plausible but not byte-level verified.

signature.mechanism_summary: VP Hamming-distance feedback rewards progress on allocation-size comparisons independently of I2S substitution; cmplog's I2S pulls the 4-byte iTXt length field back toward plausible recorded values, undoing large-length mutations that trigger the NULL allocation path.
signature.one_line: VP gradient preserves oversized iTXt length that I2S substitution reverts

analysis.summary_one_line: iTXt chunk with a length field large enough that png_read_buffer's allocation fails and returns NULL.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed PNG prelude (signature + IHDR) followed by an iTXt chunk whose 4-byte big-endian length header is set to a value large enough that the subsequent png_read_buffer(length+1, 1) call cannot allocate it (png_malloc_base returns NULL), forcing the True branch at pngrutil.c:2748. The input does not need to actually contain `length` bytes of chunk data — only the declared length field matters because the allocation is attempted before the data is read.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Both winners (naive and value_profile_cmplog) reach the buffer==NULL True branch in 8/10 trials. The winner-resolving seeds we have are 8-12 KB sized PNGs whose declared iTXt chunk length is large enough to defeat png_malloc_base; the per-role overlay shows W exclusively executing the OOM-handling block (lines 2749-2753) and never the buffer!=NULL post-buffer-read path, confirming buffer == NULL fires.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 1/10 trials and is blocked 9/10 with avg duration 10.80h. Its I2SRandReplace mutator, driven by CmpLogObserver entries on the chunk-length and chunk-type CMPs that fire during chunk-header parsing, systematically substitutes the length field back to plausible in-file values (or to constants seen in length CMPs upstream), pinning length to ranges that png_malloc_base can satisfy. The L seeds in BRANCH SEEDS take the False branch and continue into the prefix-scanning loop at L2761-L2764 and beyond — i.e., they reach the chunk with a length the allocator handled.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Composite Pattern-B story (claimed_mechanism = other; no single positive enabler explains both decisive pairs). On (naive, cmplog) the input-to-state substitution mutator that cmplog carries on top of naive's stack is counterproductive here: it scans CmpLogObserver entries from chunk-length and chunk-type comparisons and pulls the 4-byte iTXt length field back toward plausible recorded values, undoing the random-large mutations that naive's pure havoc/token stack produces freely. On (value_profile_cmplog, cmplog) the value-profile Hamming-distance feedback signal rewards progress on the allocation-size comparison path independently of the input-to-state substitutions that trap cmplog. Both decisive pairs collapse to the same single feature requirement (a huge declared iTXt length), so the technique label `other` is the honest pick — it is the I2S sub-mutator hurting cmplog that drives the divergence, not any single positive enabler shared by the two winners.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is `if (buffer == NULL)` at pngrutil.c:2748 in OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_iTXt and the globally blocked side is True.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Location: /src/libpng/pngrutil.c:2748:8
Enclosing function: OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_iTXt
Source line:    if (buffer == NULL)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: Buffer is assigned from png_read_buffer(png_ptr, length+1, 1) immediately before the blocking branch, so taking the True branch requires that allocation to fail.
Winners execute only the OOM-handling block (lines 2749-2753) and never the buffer-consumption path that follows; losers execute the post-allocation path including the prefix-scanning loop.
naive resolves 8/10 trials while cmplog resolves 1/10 and is blocked 9/10; value_profile_cmplog also resolves 8/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive                            8        2          0  winner (I2S vs cmplog)
cmplog                           1        9          0  loser (I2S vs naive); loser (value_profile vs value_profile_cmplog)
value_profile                    8        2          0  REFERENCE
value_profile_cmplog             8        2          0  winner (value_profile vs cmplog)

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs identify cmplog as the loser with the same prob_div=0.70 and the same 1/10 vs 8/10 split; both pair-level deltas are statistically significant.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
--- Pair 1: naive > cmplog  [delta: I2S] ---
  subject 9  (cmplog vs naive, admissible)
  winner: resolved=8/10  blocked=2  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=1/10  blocked=9  unreached=0
  avg duration blocked: winner=4.70h  loser=10.80h
  avg hitcount on branch: winner=1  loser=0
  prob_div=0.70  dur_div=6.10h  hit_div=1
  subject-level: delta_AUC=3983370.0  p_AUC=0.0002  delta_Final=309.8  p_final=0.0002
--- Pair 2: value_profile_cmplog > cmplog  [delta: value_profile] ---

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds proceed past the blocker into the keyword-prefix scanning loop, confirming they had a length the allocator handled.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2762  T=0 F=0  T=75 F=2  prefix_length < length && buffer[prefix_length] != 0;

evidence_trail.claim: naive's mutator stack is pure havoc/token with no I2S — it cannot CMP-anchor mutations on the chunk-length field.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Mutators**: havoc + token stack — `ByteFlipMutator`, `ByteRandMutator`,
`ByteIncMutator`, `ByteDecMutator`, `ByteAddMutator`, `WordAddMutator`,
`DwordAddMutator`, `QwordAddMutator`, `BytesDeleteMutator`,
`BytesInsertMutator`, `BytesInsertCopyMutator`, `BytesExpandMutator`,
`BytesRandInsertMutator`, `BytesRandSetMutator`, `BytesCopyMutator`,
`BytesSwapMutator`, `WordInterestingMutator`, `DwordInterestingMutator`,
`ByteInterestingMutator`, `CrossoverInsertMutator`,
`CrossoverReplaceMutator`, `TokenInsert`, `TokenReplace`.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's I2SRandReplace scans the input for byte sequences matching one CMP operand and substitutes the other, which on chunk-length CMPs pulls a havoc-mutated huge length back toward plausible recorded values.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog has both CMP_MAP gradient and I2S; the CMP_MAP gradient bucket signal lets it climb the allocation/length CMP gradient even when I2S substitutions push back.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Instrumentation**: union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A parameterized synthetic that gates a `buffer == NULL` branch behind a 4-byte big-endian length field at a fixed file offset, swept over scan_values = {0x00000100, 0x00100000, 0x10000000, 0xFFFFFFFF}, should show cmplog's resolve rate decreasing monotonically as the required length grows and diverging from naive's by >=30 percentage points at the highest scan value. If instead cmplog matches or beats naive at the high-length scan points, the I2S-anchoring story is wrong.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I cannot directly inspect the 4-byte iTXt length field in the winner seeds because BYTE DIFF only shows the first 64 bytes and the iTXt length header sits deeper in the file; the chunk-length-anchoring story is inferred from the source allocation path plus mutator-stack semantics rather than from a visible W-vs-L byte difference at the length offset.
